identity.name: ÉTAPE 22
identity.address: Avenue & Davenport (182 Davenport Rd, Lower Level)

narrative.summary: Minimalist cycling apparel + coffee shop. Design-forward retail/café hybrid. 5.0★ (29 reviews). Near John's area. Soft-opened March 2026.

blocks.title: The Space
blocks.body: You'll find it below street level at Avenue and Davenport, tucked into the lower level where minimalist cycling apparel hangs on clean white walls alongside an espresso bar. The studio feels deliberately spare—polished concrete, carefully curated bike kits from MAAP and ASSOS displayed like gallery pieces, a few stools where you can sip espresso while running your hand over Italian chamois pads. It's half retail shrine to cycling culture, half café, all designed with the kind of restraint that makes you notice every stitch.

blocks.title: The Food
blocks.body: This isn't a café menu—it's espresso, full stop, pulled by owners Mitch and Henry who know as much about extraction as they do about gear ratios. The coffee program is tight and serious, the kind of place where your cortado arrives with actual latte art and tastes like they give a damn. Expect to pay café prices (espresso drinks around $4-6), but you're here for the vibe as much as the caffeine, browsing $300 bib shorts between sips.

blocks.title: Go When
blocks.body: Stop by on a Saturday morning before or after a ride, when you want to talk cadence with people who actually understand, or on a weekday afternoon when you need to justify buying gear you don't need with excellent coffee you do.

blocks.title: The Vibe
blocks.body:
⭐⭐⭐⭐⭐ "I went to visit Etape22 in Toronto over the weekend and it is one of the best bike / cycling apparel experiences I've had. It doesn't matter if you are a beginner or expert in cycling, the owners make you feel welcome and have amazing cycling knowledge. It was really interesting learning about some of the brands they carry (MAAP, Universal Colours, ASSOS, etc.) and what makes their cycling kits unique. They also have great coffee which was yummy to sip on while perusing through their cycling apparel. Overall, one of the best cycling retail / coffee shop experiences in Toronto!"

⭐⭐⭐⭐⭐ "Both Mitch and Henry are great guys and very tentative to your orders and needs. Not only is the studio space gorgeous they also brew a great espresso ;)"

⭐⭐⭐⭐⭐ "Hidden gem in midtown! Mitch and Henry are super nice and helpful regarding everything cycling related!

Easily become my favorite cycling shop in the city!

Coffee is also great for mid/post ride stop:)"
